questions.questionText: Which of the following side effects is most commonly associated with ACE inhibitors due to bradykinin accumulation?
questions.explanation: ACE inhibitors prevent the breakdown of bradykinin, which can lead to a persistent dry cough in some patients.

questions.questionText: Spironolactone is classified as a potassium-sparing diuretic.
questions.explanation: True. Spironolactone acts as an aldosterone antagonist, promoting sodium excretion while retaining potassium.

questions.questionText: What is the primary mechanism of action of Benzodiazepines?
options: Increasing the duration of chloride channel opening
Directly opening chloride channels without GABA
Increasing the frequency of chloride channel opening
questions.explanation: Benzodiazepines enhance the effect of GABA by increasing the frequency of chloride channel opening, unlike barbiturates which increase the duration.

questions.questionText: Tetracyclines are safe for use during pregnancy and for children under 8 years old.
questions.explanation: False. Tetracyclines can cause permanent tooth discoloration and affect bone growth in fetuses and young children.

questions.questionText: Which medication is the drug of choice for treating a patient with an acute attack of gout?
questions.explanation: NSAIDs or Colchicine are used for acute attacks, while Allopurinol is used for long-term prevention (chronic management).

questions.questionText: Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs) should ideally be taken 30-60 minutes before the first meal of the day.
questions.explanation: True. PPIs work best when the proton pumps are most active, which occurs after a period of fasting (like breakfast).
